title: Continuum Anomaly To Lattice No-Go
summary: The continuum fact that a lone anomalous fermion carries a half-level parity-anomaly response and boundary mode motivates the lattice theorem that a globally consistent band structure cannot realize unmatched net chirality; the current KB now makes that motivation precise while also making explicit why it is not the lattice proof itself.
assumptions: Continuum anomaly intuition is being used as motivation, not as the full proof.
The rigorous compact-BZ proof route is now explicit locally through Friedan's spectral-bundle formulation.
regime: Conceptual bridge between relativistic field theory and lattice band theory.
scope: Lecture One motivation for the Nielsen-Ninomiya theorem.

source_anchors.notes: Lecture One explicitly motivates the lattice theorem from the chiral anomaly problem.
The actual theorem proof route is the vanishing boundary Chern number of the spectral bundle.

representation: Motivational bridge from field-theory inconsistency to lattice topological constraint, with the proof-versus-motivation boundary now explicit.
failure_modes: Motivation from anomaly does not replace the actual band-topology proof.
retrieval_hints: Use when connecting relativistic anomaly language to condensed-matter no-go results.
